t: Adopt the Swift 6 language mode incrementally PER TARGET. Set 'Swift Concurrency Checking' to 'Complete' first to get warnings, fix them, then flip 'Swift Language Mode' to Swift 6 to lock in.
In Xcode 16's SDK, all SwiftUI views are now @MainActor automatically. You can REMOVE explicit @MainActor annotations from your view types -- they're redundant.
When migrating to Swift 6, start at the UI/extension target FIRST -- not your library/framework. UI code is easiest to fix and produces fewer warnings to clean.

For unmaintained frameworks emitting non-isolated callbacks, use @preconcurrency on the protocol conformance. It generates assumeIsolated calls that trap (instead of corrupt) if the contract is violated.
Core ML stateful models with KV-cache deliver a 1.6x speedup for token generation on Mistral 7B. Just preallocate state via model.makeState() instead of input/output buffers.
iOS 18 makes many Core ML models faster automatically -- WITHOUT recompiling. The OS-level inference stack improvements apply to existing model files.
Translation API batches: Group requests by source language. Mixing German + Spanish in one batch produces nonsense. Use separate batches in the same TranslationSession.
TranslationSession is anchored to its view -- if the view disappears, the session breaks. NEVER store it in a model object or singleton.
Translation APIs DO NOT FUNCTION IN THE SIMULATOR. You must develop on iPhone, iPad, or Mac. Builds will compile but runtime calls fail silently.
For large data sets in MPS Graph, use the new strided ND-array API for Fourier transforms. Significant speedup over the previous kernel-based approach.
App Intents parameter rule: parameters that refer to entities should be ENTITIES, not IDs or names. So `OpenTrailIntent(target: TrailEntity)`, NOT `OpenTrailIntent(name: String)`.
With iOS 18 SDK, App Intents derives string-search and predicate-search from `EnumerableEntityQuery` automatically. You usually only need allEntities() -- skip the more complex protocols.
Apple Intelligence Assistant Schemas: conform with @AssistantIntent(schema: .photos.openAsset). The compiler validates your intent against the trained schema -- missing required fields fail to build.
For App Intent results to Siri, return BOTH dialog AND view: `.result(dialog: "...") { Snippet() }`. On HomePod/AirPods, dialog speaks. On screen, snippet shows. Siri picks contextually.
3D asset texture packing: combine roughness + metallic + AO into one RGB texture. Up to 40% asset size reduction PLUS lossless compression (single-channel grayscale doesn't compress).
For visionOS object tracking, your viewing-angle hint (.allAngles, .upright, .front) dramatically affects training quality. Pick correctly to skip irrelevant viewpoints during ML training.
In mixed immersion Metal apps, you MUST use the new scene-aware projection matrix (cp_view_compute_projection). The old projection matrix from compositor services no longer works.
In visionOS 2, volumes inherit min/max size FROM CONTENT FRAMES. To enable corner-handle resize, change `.frame(width: ...)` to `.frame(minWidth: ...)`. One change unlocks resize.
Writing Tools modifies your text storage IN-PLACE while active, including pushing to undo. Don't persist your text storage when isWritingToolsActive==true. Use the new will/did delegate methods.
WKWebView's Writing Tools default is .limited (panel only), NOT .complete (inline). Set writingToolsBehavior explicitly if you want the full inline experience in WebKit-based content.
Swift Testing parallelizes test functions BY DEFAULT, including parameterized cases. Each argument runs as an independent test that can re-run individually in Xcode 16.
Use #require instead of XCTUnwrap for optional values: `let v = try #require(maybeNil)`. Test stops cleanly if nil; works in async/throwing contexts.
withKnownIssue is strictly better than .disabled for flaky tests. The test still compiles and runs; expected failures don't break CI; you're alerted when the issue is fixed.
Explicitly Built Modules in Xcode 16 -- enable for Swift in build settings. Better build parallelism + LLDB reuses build modules instead of re-building (huge first-`p` speedup).
On-Demand Resources is deprecated. Migrate to Background Assets. Custom in-app purchase flows may also be affected by App Store server API v1 deprecations.
Spotlight semantic search (NEW in iOS 18) finds entities by MEANING -- searching 'pets' finds entities tagged with cats, dogs, snakes. Adopt IndexedEntity to opt your AppEntities in.
For LLDB high-firing breakpoints: a Swift Error breakpoint stops on ANY thrown error -- often more precise than ignoring counts or recompiling with conditionals.
Swift's @DebugDescription macro is strictly better than CustomDebugStringConvertible for string-interpolation descriptions. The macro produces output `p` understands without evaluating code.
For autorelease pool growth in tight loops touching Foundation/UIKit, wrap loop bodies in `autoreleasepool { ... }`. Especially critical when generating thumbnails or processing collections of files.
Lock-Screen Camera Capture extensions MUST show a viewfinder immediately at launch -- or get terminated. They also CANNOT use network or shared containers. Plan around this.
PhotoKit on the Lock Screen: when device is locked, only photos written DURING the current capture session are readable. Privacy by default -- no leak of older photos.
Automatic passkey upgrades: pass `.requestStyle = .conditional`. The system silently creates a passkey after password sign-in if conditions are met. NO upsell screen -- delete that code.

For RealityKit portal crossing in visionOS 2, use EnvironmentLightingConfigurationComponent + a 0..1 weight to smoothly blend IBL across the portal surface. Avoids the harsh light cutoff at the boundary.
Polygon budget for Vision Pro: <500K triangles for immersive scenes; <250K for shared space; aim for ~100K visible at any one time. Split large models into chunks for culling.
For accessibility on cards/rows, use .accessibilityElement(children: .combine) to merge multiple stops into one element with custom actions. Massive UX win for VoiceOver list navigation.
In iOS 18, accessibility modifiers accept an isEnabled parameter. Conditional .accessibilityLabel("Super favorite", isEnabled: isSuper) preserves the system default when not active.

t: UIScene lifecycle adoption is MANDATORY in the release following iOS 26. Apps that don't adopt it will not launch.
Data.dropFirst() is O(n), not O(1). Use Slice or the new Span type instead for zero-copy views into data.
Property declaration order in @Generable structs affects Foundation Models output quality. Put summaries LAST for better streaming UX and generation quality.
SwiftUI Lists are 6x faster for large data sets. ForEach initialization is 16x faster. These gains are automatic with no code changes.
SwiftUI's visualEffect modifier runs on background threads. Never access @MainActor state inside it or you'll get concurrency violations.
For libraries, prefer 'nonisolated' over '@concurrent'. Let the caller decide whether to offload work to a background thread.
SceneKit is officially soft-deprecated. RealityKit with ECS architecture is the path forward. Start migration planning now.
session.prewarm(promptPrefix:) can eliminate Foundation Models asset loading latency. Call it when the user navigates to a screen that will use AI.
includeSchemaInPrompt = false saves hundreds of tokens per request when you've already provided examples in instructions.
Quantum-secure TLS is enabled by DEFAULT in iOS 26. Your app gets post-quantum protection automatically if using URLSession/Network.framework.
Observable models don't need @MainActor annotation when main actor mode is on. Swift infers isolation from the instantiation context.
On-Demand Resources is deprecated. Background Assets with 200GB Apple hosting replaces it completely.
Eytzinger layout for binary search is dramatically more cache-friendly than sorted arrays. Apple engineers recommend it for hot lookup paths.
In Swift 6.2, async functions no longer eagerly offload. A nonisolated async function called from @MainActor stays on the main actor unless marked @concurrent.
App Intents now support the 'Use Model' Shortcuts action, letting users build AI-powered automations that run your app's Foundation Models sessions.
Profile BEFORE adding concurrency. Many perceived concurrency needs are actually algorithmic inefficiencies that should be fixed first.
PermissionKit models child safety as 'permission as a feature, not a gate' -- parents approve specific communication partners.
The new SwiftUI Instrument shows cause-and-effect graphs: which state change caused which view body evaluation. Essential for performance debugging.
